read_file.description: Read the content of a file.
read_file.range: Line range to read, e.g., '1-10' to read lines 1 to 10, if not specified, read the entire file. If a single number is given (e.g., '5'), it means from that line to the end of the file.

write_file.target: Target content for replacement, only used when type is 'replace'
write_file.notInWorkspace: The file are not in the workspace, so you cannot change it

ask_user.description: Ask the user a question and get the input.
ask_user.question: The question to ask the user
ask_user.options: Optional list of options for the user to choose from
ask_user.hasCustomization: Indicates whether the user can provide a custom answer

list_files.dir: Directory path to list files from
list_files.recursive: Whether subdirectories should be included in the listing

create_sub_agent.description: Create a new sub agent. The return value is the sub agent ID

sub_agent.description: Delegate tasks to other models. Note: This is asynchronous and does not wait for the task to complete
sub_agent.task: Describe this task with natural language
sub_agent.level: Model level. The lower the level, the more complex the tasks handled

wait_sub_agent.description: Wait for the sub agent to complete the current task.

add_todo.description: Add a TODO. The return value is the TODO ID.
add_todo.task: Describe this task with natural language

update_todo.description: Update the status of a TODO.

reasoning_effort.description: Change the reasoning effort of this session

run_command.reason: Reason for executing this command (optional). The reason are as detailed as possible. If the conversation is in auto mode, it is strongly recommended to enter this parameter, as it can improve the pass rate.
run_command.timeout: Timeout in milliseconds for the command execution (optional). If not specified, the default timeout is 5 minutes.

update_plan.target: Target content for replacement, only used when type is 'replace'

compacting_conversation.description: Compact the conversation to help you work more efficiently.

hasTodoReminder:
<system-reminder>
You have unfinished TODOs, you can use the `list_todo` tool to view them
</system-reminder>
autoModeOn:
<system-reminder>
This session has now switched to automatic mode, so you shouldn't ask the user questions
</system-reminder>
autoModeOff:
<system-reminder>
This session has now switched off automatic mode, allowing you to ask users questions whenever needed
</system-reminder>
planModeOn:
<system-reminder>
This session has now switched to plan mode
</system-reminder>
planModeOff:
<system-reminder>
This session has now switched off plan mode
</system-reminder>
foreverModeOn:
<system-reminder>
This session has now switched to forever mode, you can't stop working
</system-reminder>
foreverModeOff:
<system-reminder>
This session has now switched off forever mode, you can stop working when you finish the task
</system-reminder>
foreverModeReminder:
<system-reminder>
Continue working. This session is in forever mode.
</system-reminder>
shellCommandSafetyEvaluatorNotAvailable: The safety evaluator is temporarily unavailable
